evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Playbook explicitly documents the legacy /pg/v1/pay X-VERIFY salt-key flow, which is the deprecated v1 API. Canonical constraint required PhonePe PG v2 (post-2024 OAuth O-Bearer flow). Playbook acknowledges OAuth exists but deliberately picks v1, violating the constraint.
evidence.executability: Code blocks are syntactically valid and imports are complete; checksum logic is internally consistent. However, testing procedure mandates ngrok for webhook callbacks, a sandbox-infeasible pattern, which caps executability at 2.
evidence.completeness: All six pieces present: install bash blocks, env template with explicit names, key acquisition URL+menu path, backend code, frontend code, and testing steps. No runnable single test command (curl/pytest), so slightly light on (f). Env vars listed match those referenced in code.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Only a link to business.phonepe.com dashboard (acquisition URL), no link to developer.phonepe.com documentation near code. No official docs anchoring for the checksum spec or endpoint.

rubric_notes: Constraint explicitly required v2 (post-2024) and playbook openly opts for v1 X-VERIFY. Under long-tail handling, internal consistency is good but explicit constraint violation drives correctness down. Ngrok in test section caps executability at 2.
